title: macOS launcher: drop the broken no-terminal .app, keep start.command, add setup.command for Gatekeeper
description: ORIGINAL GOAL: give macOS a no-terminal default launcher (like Windows start.vbs / Linux detached start.sh) via a CubricVision.app bundle. OUTCOME (2026-06-10, M4): the .app DID NOT WORK. Finder launched it under Rosetta (x86) and it crashed with `EPERM: process.cwd failed (uv_cwd)` — a .app runs with cwd `/`, so the bundled Electron could not resolve the portable root. Reverted to the proven start.command (terminal launcher, validated since 0.0.8). RESOLUTION: the real first-launch blocker on mac is plain Gatekeeper QUARANTINE on the download (unsigned/un-notarized) — confirmed on M4: start.command launches clean after `xattr -dr com.apple.quarantine`. The per-launch xattr inside start.command can't beat it (Gatekeeper blocks before the script runs). Fix shipped: a one-time setup.command (mac-only) the user right-click->Open's once; it strips quarantine on the whole folder, then start.command launches by double-click. Wired into build-portable.mjs (PLATFORM_CONFIG.darwin `setup` field, staged into full + update bundle + manifest launchers) + documented in the macOS README + docs site. Shipped in 0.0.12 (the .app was briefly in 0.0.11; 0.0.12 removes it). A proper no-terminal mac launch (signed .app) is deferred and needs a paid Apple Developer account for notarization.
